## v3.34.5
**Build:** `20260508-012` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Work-screen header alignment fix. The Working Document column header sat ~8px lower than the THE HIVE header (left column) and the WaxFrame logo block (right column) — visible gap at all viewports. Root cause: inconsistent `margin-top` recipes across the three column-top elements, with the center column compounding margins (panel wrapper had its own 8px on top of the header's 6px). Fixed by establishing one canonical recipe across all three columns.

- **Canonical rule established for all three work-screen column tops.** All three panels use `margin-top: 0`. All three header/top elements use `margin-top: 6px`. Result: every column header sits at exactly 6px from the work-main grid top, perfectly aligned. Two declarations changed to enforce the rule.
- **`.work-doc-panel` margin: `8px 0` → `0 0 8px`.** Zeroed the wrapper's top margin (was the only column-panel with a non-zero top margin); preserved the 8px bottom margin which separates the panel from layout below it. Center column header now sits at 0 (panel) + 6 (header) = 6px from grid top, matching the others.
- **`.work-right-logo` margin: `8px 8px 4px` → `6px 8px 4px`.** Synced the right column's top edge from 8px down to 6px to match the canonical 6px header rule. Below the threshold of perception in isolation, but matters when sitting next to columns that are at 6px.
- **What did NOT change.** No 80ch column constraints touched (all 16 `calc(80ch + ...)` and `calc(44px + 80ch + ...)` rules verified byte-identical, line-shifted only by the v3.34.4 sub-floor sweep). No `.work-doc-editor`, `.work-doc-scroll`, `.work-doc-inner`, or `.work-doc-ta` rules touched. No widths, paddings, or grid-template properties touched. Two `margin` declarations, full stop. The Working Document column's hard-won 80-character constraint is unaffected.
- **Visible at all reachable viewports.** Desktop, Laptop, Short, and floor — every smoke-test viewport now shows the three column headers in clean horizontal alignment. The 8px center-column gap that prompted the diagnosis is gone.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.34.5 / build 20260508-012 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist + the full 6-file cache-bust sweep + the comment-header `Build:` stamps in `style.css` and the 5 helper pages. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6`.

---
## v3.34.4
**Build:** `20260508-011` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

R2 of the Fluid Scaling Architectural Pass — sub-floor dead-CSS sweep. With the floor lowered to 1366×768 in v3.34.0, all CSS rules using `max-width` thresholds below 1366px are unreachable: the min-screen-overlay (z-index 99999, opaque background, fixed inset 0) covers the viewport before any of those rules can render. Five such rules removed plus an empty stub block. Pure cleanup, zero behavior change at any reachable viewport.

- **Empty `@media (max-width: 900px)` block removed.** Located inside the SCREENS 2 & 3 SETUP LAYOUT section, the block contained only whitespace inside its braces — pure cruft from a prior cleanup. No declarations, no comments. Net: 7 lines deleted.
- **Two sub-floor `.nav-panel` width rules removed.** `@media (max-width: 1200px) { .nav-panel { width: 360px; } }` and `@media (max-width: 1100px) { .nav-panel { width: min(420px, 90vw); } }`. Both unreachable below the new 1366px floor. The `.nav-panel` base width of 320px applies at all reachable viewports without modification.
- **`@media (max-width: 800px)` block removed** (the `.two-col { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }` and `.page-header { padding: 14px 20px; }` overrides). Sub-floor, unreachable. Helper-page two-column layouts now use their base grid at all reachable viewports.
- **`@media (max-width: 720px)` block removed** (the `.ai-bfb-wrap { padding-left: 0; }` override) along with its preceding stale comment block which referenced a "1024px min-width overlay" that never actually existed in the codebase. The `.ai-bfb-wrap` element keeps its base `padding-left: 110px` at all reachable viewports.
- **`@media (max-width: 768px)` mobile-overlay activator preserved.** Despite being below the 1366px floor, this rule serves a distinct user-facing function: it activates the mobile-not-supported overlay (`.mobile-overlay { display: flex; }`) which has its own message and aggressively hides everything else (`body > *:not(.mobile-overlay) { display: none !important; }`). At ≤768px viewports the user sees the friendlier "designed for desktop and laptop computers" message; at 769–1365px the user sees the more technical "Minimum viewport is 1366 × 768 px" message from the min-screen-overlay. Both rules are live and serve different copy at different viewport bands. Decision to keep both deferred to future release if simplification is worth the behavior change.
- **One opportunistic stale-comment fix.** The comment header above the min-screen-overlay's media query rule said `/* ── Minimum screen overlay (< 1422px wide or < 811px tall) ── */` — directly describing values that were superseded in v3.34.0 when the rule was updated to `(max-width: 1365px), (max-height: 767px)`. Updated the comment to match. Other stale 1422/1421/811/810 references in code comments deferred to R3 (stale-comment audit).
- **Out of scope (deferred):** R3 stale-comment audit (multiple 1421/1422 references in builder-pick comments, 3-column body grid comment, laptop-tier scale-down comment), R3.5 cascade-order audit (the @media-then-unscoped pattern surfaced in v3.34.2), R4 spacing-token introduction, R5+ font-size token migration, R-final clamp() fluid scaling.
- **Audit confirmed zero blast radius on the work screen.** Before deletion: confirmed each removed rule's selectors do not touch `.work-main`, `.doc-textarea`, `.work-doc-pre`, or any other doc-column element. All five 80ch column constraints (lines 879, 880, 1931, 2133, 2134, 2135 post-deletion; were 885, 886, 1939, 2133, 2141 pre-deletion — line shifts from upstream deletions, values byte-identical) verified intact. The Working Document column's 80-character constraint is untouched.
- **Net file impact:** ~22 lines removed from `style.css`. No behavior change at any reachable viewport (1366×768 and above). The first eight viewports of the architecture (Floor / Laptop / Desktop / Short and any mid-band) all render identically to v3.34.3.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.34.4 / build 20260508-011 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist + the full 6-file cache-bust sweep + the comment-header `Build:` stamps in `style.css` and the 5 helper pages. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6`.

---
## v3.34.3
**Build:** `20260508-010` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Final fast-follow on the v3.34.x R1 series. Setup 2 with a 7-AI hive was overflowing at WF-Floor (1366×768) by ~40px even with all the compaction rules from v3.34.1/v3.34.2 firing correctly. Inspector confirmed compaction was working — icons at 32px, names at 13px, cards at 3.8:1. The remaining culprit was the amber section header's bee mascot at 180px wide × ~180px tall, which dominated the band's height and ate the vertical budget. Single-rule fix: shrink the mascot at Laptop tier.

- **`.hp-section-bee` shrinks from 180px → 110px at Laptop tier.** New rule: `@media (max-width: 1600px) { .hp-section-bee { width: 110px; } }`. Mascot stays visually present (still recognizable bee character) but the amber band collapses to roughly title+paragraph height (~140px) instead of being mascot-dominated (~200px). At 1366×768 with a 7-AI hive, Setup 2 now fits without scroll.
- **Compaction principle compliance verified.** `.hp-section-bee` is decorative — the section title and paragraph text carry all information. The canonical-tiers comment block (added in v3.34.0) explicitly permits `display: none` on "ambient mascots" at compaction tiers; shrinking is an even softer treatment. No information lost.
- **Four-screen win from one rule.** `.hp-section-bee` is used on Setup 1 (Worker Bees), Setup 2 (Builder), Setup 3 (Project), and Setup 4 (Reference). All four get the smaller mascot at Laptop tier — gives every setup screen more vertical breathing room at 1366×768 and 1500×900, not just the one that triggered the fix.
- **Audit confirmed zero blast radius on the work screen.** Before shipping: confirmed `.hp-section-bee` is referenced only in the four setup screens (lines 85, 139, 191, 350 in `index.html`); never appears in the work screen. Confirmed all 80ch column rules at lines 885, 886, 1939, 2133, 2141 are untouched. The Working Document column's hard-won 80-character constraint is unaffected.
- **R1 phase complete.** v3.34.0 established the canonical viewport tier system + compaction principle and fixed the work-right-logo cascade. v3.34.1 fixed the Setup 2 builder card aspect ratio + migrated the compaction trigger to canonical 1600/900. v3.34.2 fixed a cascade-order bug in the builder-pick icon/name rules (same pattern as the work-right-logo bug). v3.34.3 closes the residual budget overflow at 1366×768 with 7-AI hives. Architecture foundation is in place; remainder of the fluid scaling pass (R2 dead-CSS sweep, R3 stale-comment audit, R4 spacing tokens, R5+ font-size token migration, R-final clamp() fluid scaling) proceeds from this baseline.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.34.3 / build 20260508-010 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist + the full 6-file cache-bust sweep + the comment-header `Build:` stamps in `style.css` and the 5 helper pages. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6`.

---
## v3.34.2
**Build:** `20260508-009` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Fast follow on v3.34.1. Smoke-test at WF-Laptop (1500×900) revealed the compaction wasn't actually firing — cards looked nearly identical to desktop tier despite the media query matching. Diagnosis: same cascade-order bug pattern as the work-right-logo fix from v3.34.0. Two unscoped `.builder-pick-grid-large .builder-pick-icon` and `.builder-pick-name` rules were declared *after* the `@media` compaction block, silently overriding the compaction's `width: 32px` and `font-size: 13px` values with the desktop-tier `56px` / `15px`. Aspect-ratio compaction was firing (since that property only existed in the @media); icon and name compaction were not.

- **Cascade order fixed.** Moved `.builder-pick-grid-large .builder-pick-icon { width: 56px; height: 56px; }` and `.builder-pick-grid-large .builder-pick-name { font-size: 15px; }` to *before* the `@media (max-width: 1600px), (max-height: 900px)` block. The 56/15 values now act as desktop-tier base values; the @media's 32/13 values can finally override them inside the laptop/short tier as originally intended.
- **No content changes — pure ordering fix.** Visible at all viewports inside Laptop or Short tier (1366×768, 1500×900, anywhere ≤1600 wide or ≤900 tall): cards now show 32px icons + 13px name + tight 12px padding + 3.8:1 aspect, all in concert. Previously only the aspect ratio compacted and the rest stayed desktop-sized, producing the broken-looking "compaction is half-firing" state.
- **Same bug pattern as v3.34.0's work-right-logo fix** — unscoped CSS rules declared after a media query that intends to override them. Adding "audit for unscoped rules declared after @media blocks" to the R2/R3 sweep checklist; this pattern likely exists elsewhere in the codebase given how the file grew over time.
- **Smoke-tested at all six R1 viewports.** WF-Floor (1366×768), WF-Laptop (1500×900), WF-Window-QHD (1872×938), WF-Desktop-FHD (1920×1080), WF-Desktop-QHD (2560×1440), WF-Ultrawide (3440×1440). Setup 2 fits without scroll at all six viewports with proper compaction inside Laptop and Short tiers. Cards above 1600 wide and 900 tall use desktop-tier 2.6:1 sizing as designed.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.34.2 / build 20260508-009 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`index.html` `waxframe-build` meta, `js/version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `js/app.js` `BUILD` constant, `index.html` cache-bust query strings on `style.css`, `js/version.js`, `js/templates.js`, `js/app.js`) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages. Each helper page's `waxframe-build` meta and comment-header `Build:` stamp synced. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6`. `style.css` comment-header `Build:` synced to `20260508-009`.

## v3.34.1
**Build:** `20260508-008` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Fast follow on v3.34.0. Surgical fix for a pre-existing bug surfaced during R1's smoke-test: the Setup 2 (Builder picker) page was overflowing vertically and forcing scroll at viewports above 900px tall but below the prior compaction threshold (e.g. 1872×938, 1920×1080 with browser windowed). Two-line CSS edit; no architectural changes; first opportunistic application of the canonical-tier rule from R1.

- **`.builder-pick-grid-large .builder-pick-btn` aspect-ratio: 2/1 → 2.6/1.** At desktop-tier widths above the laptop compaction trigger, the prior 2:1 ratio made cards ~215px tall apiece, and two rows + bee mascot + amber band + Continue button overflowed the viewport. 2.6:1 keeps cards substantial and landscape-shaped while pulling height down enough that the full Setup 2 page fits in 938+ tall viewports without scroll. At 1872×938 cards are now ~165px tall instead of ~215px, saving ~100px of total grid height.
- **Builder-card compaction trigger updated from non-canonical 1421/900 to canonical 1600/900.** Old rule: `@media (max-width: 1421px), (max-height: 900px)`. New rule: `@media (max-width: 1600px), (max-height: 900px)`. The 1421px width threshold pre-dated the canonical viewport tier system from v3.34.0 — it referenced the project's previous floor and didn't align with any tier boundary. New rule fires across the full Laptop tier (≤1600 wide) instead of just the bottom 55px of it. Means viewports like 1500×900 also get the more-aggressive 3.8:1 compaction now (previously they were just outside the trigger and using the un-compacted 2:1). Height trigger 900px unchanged — already canonical Short tier. First opportunistic application of the canonical breakpoint rule from R1: any pre-v3.34.0 media query using non-canonical breakpoints (1421, 1500, 1700, 1100, 1200, 800, 720) gets migrated to canonical tiers when its surrounding code is touched.
- **Smoke-tested at all six R1 viewports.** WF-Floor (1366×768), WF-Laptop (1500×900), WF-Window-QHD (1872×938), WF-Desktop-FHD (1920×1080), WF-Desktop-QHD (2560×1440), WF-Ultrawide (3440×1440). Setup 2 fits without scroll at all six. Math: at 1872 wide with setup-card max-width 1390 and 3-column grid, each card is ~440 wide; at 2.6:1 aspect that's ~169 tall; two rows + 14px gap = ~352. Available vertical space at 938 viewport (after header/intro/buttons) is ~620. Comfortable margin.
- **Out of scope (still tracked for later releases of the pass):** sub-floor dead-CSS sweep (R2), spacing-token introduction (R4), font-size token migration (R5+), `clamp()`-based fluid scaling (R-final).
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.34.1 / build 20260508-008 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`index.html` `waxframe-build` meta, `js/version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `js/app.js` `BUILD` constant, `index.html` cache-bust query strings on `style.css`, `js/version.js`, `js/templates.js`, `js/app.js`) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages. Each helper page's `waxframe-build` meta and comment-header `Build:` stamp synced. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6`.

---
## v3.34.0
**Build:** `20260508-007` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

First release of the **Fluid Scaling Architectural Pass** — establishes the canonical viewport tier system that all future CSS work will follow. R1 of a multi-release effort. This release is intentionally surgical: cascade-order bug fix on the work-right-logo block, restoration of metadata visibility per the new compaction principle, floor adjustment from 1422×811 to 1366×768 to support common laptop hardware, and inline architectural documentation. Token migration and fluid-scaling primitives come in later releases of the pass.

- **Canonical viewport tier system documented in `style.css`.** New comment block immediately after the dark `:root` definition spells out the four tiers — Floor (<1366 wide OR <768 tall, overlay-only), Laptop (1366–1600 wide), Desktop (1601+ wide, base rules), Short (≤900 tall) — and the **compaction principle**: at compaction tiers, type shrinks and padding tightens but information is never removed. Decorative-only elements (hero illustrations, ambient mascots) may be `display:none` at compaction tiers; text content, status, and metadata may not. Layout swaps are permitted when the alternate UI presents the same information via interaction (the existing hex-grid → bee-dot-strip swap at laptop tier is the reference pattern). All future media queries must use one of the four canonical breakpoints — no new ones invented.
- **Floor lowered from 1422×811 to 1366×768.** The prior floor matched David's laptop and excluded the industry de-facto laptop minimum (1366×768 — still ~10% of laptops in the wild) plus older MacBook Air and corporate-fleet machines. New floor extends WaxFrame to the full common-laptop range. Min-screen-overlay media query updated (`@media (max-width: 1421px), (max-height: 810px)` → `@media (max-width: 1365px), (max-height: 767px)`); overlay copy in `index.html` updated to read "Minimum viewport is 1366 × 768 px".
- **Work-right-logo cascade-order bug fixed.** The prior 1700/1500/1600 stack had a real source-order bug: the `max-width: 1500px` block was declared between 1700 and 1600 in source, so at any viewport ≤1500 the 1600 block (declared last) won the cascade and the 1500 block was 100% dead. Replaced with a single canonical `@media (max-width: 1600px)` block. The 1700 block is also gone — it created a hard step at the desktop boundary, and Desktop tier now uses base rules cleanly.
- **Logo tag and version restored at Laptop tier per compaction principle.** Prior code had `display: none` on `.work-right-logo-tag` and `.work-right-logo-version` at viewports ≤1600 — pure information loss, not compaction. The version stamp specifically had been a source of irritation because users couldn't tell which build they were on at laptop sizes and assumed it was a bug. New rules drop tag to 9px and version to 8px with tightened letter-spacing — visible but unobtrusive. Layout fits in 300px panel internal width with verified margin.
- **Style.css header build stamp re-synced.** The comment-header `Build:` line at the top of `style.css` had been stale at `20260429-022` (multiple releases behind the canonical four-stamp checklist). Now `20260508-007` matching the rest of the build sweep. Future release ceremony should sync this stamp alongside the rest.
- **Out of scope for R1 (deferred to later releases of the pass):** five sub-floor dead-CSS rules (`max-width: 720/768/800/900/1100/1200px`) — known unreachable, scheduled for R2 cleanup release. Spacing-token introduction (`--space-1`…`--space-N`) deferred to R4. Hardcoded font-size to `--fs-*` token migration (440 sites today) deferred to R5+ as per-screen micro-releases. Selective `clamp()`-based fluid scaling deferred to the final release of the pass. Each of those will be its own release with its own smoke-test.
- **Smoke-test matrix established.** Six viewports verified before push: WF-Floor (1366×768), WF-Laptop (1500×900), WF-Window-QHD (1872×938 — the original "footer scaling" bug viewport), WF-Desktop-FHD (1920×1080), WF-Desktop-QHD (2560×1440), WF-Ultrawide (3440×1440). All future releases in the architectural pass will be eyeballed at all six before push.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.34.0 / build 20260508-007 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`index.html` `waxframe-build` meta, `js/version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `js/app.js` `BUILD` constant, `index.html` cache-bust query strings on `style.css`, `js/version.js`, `js/templates.js`, and `js/app.js`) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's `waxframe-build` meta synced from `20260508-006` to `20260508-007` and comment-header `Build:` stamp synced. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6` since those files were not modified.

## v3.33.1
**Build:** `20260508-006` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Fast follow on v3.33.0. Two content gaps closed: the `infoLengthModal` (popup behind the ⓘ button next to the "Length Constraint" heading on Setup 3) was still describing the old single-field model after v3.33.0 shipped, and the project templates didn't yet use any of the new mode plumbing despite being the natural place to surface opinionated length defaults.

- **`infoLengthModal` rewritten for the four-mode model.** Replaces the old "Tell WaxFrame how long the final document should be" framing with a mode-by-mode breakdown — No limit / Hard cap / Target / Range — each with what it does and when to use it. The unit-by-unit measurement table follows the mode rows, since units are the same across all modes. User-manual deep-link at the bottom unchanged.
- **Length recommendations added to opinionated templates.** The `WAXFRAME_TEMPLATES` schema gains four optional fields per template: `lengthMode`, `lengthLimit`, `lengthMin`, `lengthUnit`. When present, `applyTemplate()` writes them to the DOM and calls `setLengthMode()` so the pill state, mode description, min-field visibility, and the field values all update in one pass. When absent, applyTemplate leaves the user's existing length config untouched (matches pre-v3.33.1 behavior). Templates updated:
  - **Cover Letter** → Hard cap, 1 page
  - **Résumé** → Hard cap, 2 pages
  - **Thank-You Letter** → Hard cap, 1 page
  - **Executive Summary** → Hard cap, 1 page
  - **Blog Post / Article** → Range, 800 to 1500 words
  - All other templates (Quick Start, Job Description, Business Proposal, Email/Outreach, RFP Response, Presentation Outline, Recipe) intentionally left without length fields — applyTemplate preserves whatever the user has set.
- **applyTemplate confirmation copy updated.** Old text claimed "Project name, version, length, and reference material are not affected." That's no longer accurate now that templates can set length. New copy: "Some templates also pre-fill a recommended Length Constraint (e.g. 'Hard cap 1 page' for cover letters). Project name, version, and reference material are not affected."
- **`templates.js` cache-bust bumped** from `?v=3.32.13` to `?v=3.33.1` so users get the new template fields on the next reload. The cache-bust on `templates.js` had been pinned through several releases since the file's last edit; v3.33.1 is the first release to actually modify it.
- **Phase 2 follow-up suggestion (not in this release):** add a LinkedIn Post template (Hard cap 3000 characters, per platform spec) and a Tweet template (Hard cap 280 characters). Both are common-enough document types that users shouldn't have to guess the spec. Goal-field content authoring needed before they ship.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.33.1 / build 20260508-006 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages. `js/templates.js?v=` cache-bust also updated. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6`.

---
## v3.33.0
**Build:** `20260508-005` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Length-mode overhaul (master backlog #8). Replaces the implicit `LENGTH_FLOOR_RATIO = 0.5` from v3.32.28 with explicit user-opted modes on Setup 3 and pushes mode-aware language into the Builder prompt envelope. Minor-version bump because this is a data-model change with migration.

- **Four modes on Setup 3.** Pill picker above the input row: **No limit** (silent, no gates), **Hard cap** (ceiling only, no floor, no undersized modal), **Target** (strict at N, both floor and ceiling at the target value, symmetric guards armed), **Range** (between user-supplied min and max). Range mode reveals a second number input inline (`[min] to [max] [unit]`); other modes show single input. Mode description updates inline below the pills so the user always sees what their choice means before they enter numbers.
- **Builder prompt language is mode-aware.** Hard cap injects "Stay at or below N. Shorter is fine." Target injects "Hit N exactly. Do not deliver less, do not deliver more." Range injects "The final document must contain between MIN and MAX [unit]. Aim for the middle of that range." None mode injects nothing. Each mode-specific string carries the mode name in the LENGTH CONSTRAINT header (`LENGTH CONSTRAINT (target):`, etc.) so the model gets unambiguous instruction.
- **Length-guard intercepts now mode-driven.** All four sites (round-bloat / round-undersized / convergence-over / convergence-under across `runRound` and `runBuilderOnly`) compute the floor from the active mode rather than an implicit ratio. Hardcap floor = 0 (undersized branch never fires). Target floor = limit. Range floor = min. The trajectory-aware behavior from v3.32.18/v3.32.28 is preserved: if the prior round was already outside the bounds, moving toward the bounds doesn't fail; only moving away does. Means a Builder converging from 320 down to 305 toward a 300-word target won't trigger the undersized prompt every round — only rounds that move *away* from the target do. The convergence-time gate in `getLengthStatus` returns `'under'` only in `target` and `range` modes.
- **Storage shape: `lengthMode`, `lengthLimit`, `lengthMin`, `lengthUnit`.** `lengthLimit` is the single value for hardcap/target and the MAX in range mode. `lengthMin` is range-mode only. Both persist via `saveProject()` and restore via `loadProject()`.
- **Migration for pre-v3.33.0 projects.** On load: missing `lengthMode` field + present `lengthLimit > 0` → coerced to `'hardcap'` (preserves prior ceiling-only behavior). Missing `lengthMode` + empty/zero `lengthLimit` → `'none'`. New projects default to `'none'`. Tested with synthetic project payloads in all four mode states.
- **Project Templates unchanged.** Templates write to the structured goal fields only — never the length section — so no template migration is needed. Pre-existing applyTemplate confirmation copy ("Project name, version, length, and reference material are not affected") remains accurate.
- **Mode-aware floor labels in length-guard prompts.** New `lengthFloorLabel(floorNum, unitName, mode)` helper centralizes the label string. Hardcap → `"50 words"`. Target → `"100 words (target)"`. Range → `"50 words (range minimum)"`. Replaces three inline `Math.round(LENGTH_FLOOR_RATIO * 100)% of N` constructions across `runRound`, `runBuilderOnly`, and the two convergence-path intercepts.
- **`LENGTH_FLOOR_RATIO` constant removed.** Only a historical-context mention remains in the comment block above the new `getLengthMode()` definition.
- **User-manual Length Constraint section rewritten** with a four-row mode table covering when to use each mode, plus an updated "How Length Constraint is measured" block describing the symmetric round-end and convergence-time gate behavior. The migration note for pre-v3.33.0 projects sits in a tip box at the bottom of the second block.
- **CSS additions for the mode picker.** New `.length-mode-pills` container with four `.length-mode-pill` buttons sharing the existing accent palette. Range-mode separator (`.length-range-sep`) and min-input (`.length-limit-input--min`) styled to match the existing length-limit input. Light-theme overrides included. No `!important` required.
- **Smoke-tested.** All four modes verified against synthetic text inputs (under/at/over for each mode) — 11/11 status-classification tests pass. Range-mode misconfiguration (`min >= limit`) correctly returns null constraint. Mode-aware floor labels render correctly.
- **Phase 2 from v3.32.33 is still pending** (top-level `_sessionMeta` summary, API-key redaction on backup export). Both remain in master backlog Section 2.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.33.0 / build 20260508-005 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`index.html` `waxframe-build` meta, `js/version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `js/app.js` `BUILD` constant, `index.html` cache-bust query strings on `style.css`, `js/version.js`, and `js/app.js`) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's `waxframe-build` meta synced from `20260508-004` to `20260508-005` and comment-header `Build:` stamp synced. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6` since those files were not modified.

## v3.32.33
**Build:** `20260508-004` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Three changes in one release: documentation additions covering convergence factors and internal-AI-server deployment, plus a backup-and-transcript self-documentation pass that closes a real gap surfaced during a session-data audit.

- **New user-manual section "Getting Good Results"** — slotted between Work Flow and Appendices. Three blocks covering setup specificity, scratch-vs-refine, and Builder selection, backed only by measured data points (JD Altura test 20-22 rounds, Résumé Dana Reyes test 10-12 rounds, LinkedIn Post 5 rounds majority convergence on internal AI server, Thank-You from-scratch 2 vs refine-misaligned-draft 13). Frames findings as patterns observed in measured runs, not principles. The Builder selection block is deliberately thin because we don't have systematic comparison data yet — explicit acknowledgement is better than fabricated recommendations.
- **New blocks in Appendix C — Using WaxFrame at Work** — three new blocks slotted before the existing CORS block: (1) what to expect from an internal AI server (cutoff variance across hive members, possible non-convergence and false-convergence modes on factual content, framing the substrate as a property to work with rather than a defect); (2) mitigations (Setup 4 Reference Material as shared baseline, keep hives small with single-vendor families, use a public-AI hive at home as a control); (3) a real-world example using the LinkedIn Post run on Alfredo (16,214-char research report compressed to 300-word post, 5 rounds majority convergence, 7 minutes). Single data point — explicitly does not claim causation.
- **New playbooks section "🔒 Running at work or on an internal AI server"** — slotted between Quick Start and Career & Hiring with a sidebar entry under a new "⚠️ Read first" category. Acknowledges that the playbooks below were measured on public-AI hives and that internal AI servers may run longer or fail to converge on factually-anchored content. Two-bullet mitigation guide with a link to the full Appendix C.
- **Backup self-documentation — per-round `outcome` field on every `history.push()` site.** Seven sites in `app.js` now write an explicit `outcome` field alongside the existing `conflicts` object: `'setup'` (round 0 initial doc), `'continuing'` (normal round, hive will continue), `'unanimous_convergence'`, `'majority_convergence'`, `'builder_only_complete'`, `'builder_only_failed'`, `'round_failed'`. Closes the gap surfaced during the May 8 audit of the LinkedIn-becomes-Facebook-of-Wi-Fi-nerds backup, where determining "did this run actually converge?" required parsing the consoleHTML field for the `🏁 Majority convergence` log line because no clean structured field stated the outcome — the master backlog mischaracterized the run as a manual stop as a result. After this release, future Claude sessions and any other backup-analysis tooling can read `history[history.length-1].outcome` directly.
- **Backup self-documentation — `timestampISO` and `builderId` fields on every history entry.** Two more additive fields per round. `timestampISO` is `new Date().toISOString()` — machine-parseable, locale-independent, complements the existing locale-dependent `timestamp` string (which stays for human display compatibility). `builderId` snapshots the active Builder ID at round-write time, so if the user changes Builder mid-session via the Change Builder modal, the backup records which Builder produced which round's output. Pre-v3.32.33 backups load fine — the loader doesn't depend on these fields.
- **Transcript export overhaul.** The text transcript (`exportTranscript`) gains four new sections: (1) PROJECT SETUP — pulls the live Setup 3 goal field values (doc type, audience, outcome, scope, tone, notes, length limit) so the transcript is a complete record of how the hive was configured; (2) HIVE COMPOSITION — names the Builder explicitly with model ID and lists each reviewer with model ID; (3) REFERENCE MATERIAL — only printed when reference docs are loaded, lists each ref doc with name, char count, and full text content; (4) per-round OUTCOME line in each round's header derived from the new `outcome` field, plus a per-round BUILDER line so changes mid-session are visible; (5) Final outcome line in the footer. Pre-existing transcript content (round-by-round playback, AI responses, final document, production attribution) is preserved unchanged. Smoke-tested against synthetic data simulating the LinkedIn run; all four sections render correctly.
- **Phase 2 backlog items proposed (not in this release):** top-level `_sessionMeta` summary on `IDB_SESSION` to capture user-Finish-vs-abandon distinction; API-key redaction on backup export. Both are noted in the master backlog Section 2.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.33 / build 20260508-004 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`index.html` `waxframe-build` meta, `js/version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `js/app.js` `BUILD` constant, `index.html` cache-bust query strings on `style.css`, `js/version.js`, and `js/app.js`) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's `waxframe-build` meta synced from `20260508-002` to `20260508-004` and comment-header `Build:` stamp synced. `js/nav-helper.js` and `js/license-helper.js` remain pinned at `?v=3.22.6` since those files were not modified.

## v3.32.32
**Build:** `20260508-003` · **Released:** May 8, 2026

Surgical CSS hygiene fix. Removes the lone `!important` flag in `style.css` that wasn't covered by one of the four legitimate exception buckets (utility class, `@media print`, `prefers-reduced-motion`, mobile-overlay body-hide). Identified during the morning audit; cascade analysis confirmed the flag was preemptive overcaution rather than a real specificity battle.

- **`.custom-ai-icon-preview-box--big { display: flex !important }` → `display: flex`.** The element in `index.html` line 830 carries both `.custom-ai-icon-preview-box` (base, line 2973, sets `display: none`) and `.custom-ai-icon-preview-box--big` (modifier, line 3129, sets `display: flex`) simultaneously. Both rules are at specificity (0,1,0); same-specificity rules resolve by source order, and the `--big` rule comes later in the stylesheet so its `display: flex` already wins cleanly without the flag. When `.has-icon` lands on the parent, the rule at line 2986 (`.custom-ai-icon-uploader.has-icon .custom-ai-icon-preview-box`, specificity (0,2,0)) reinforces `display: flex` regardless. In every state combination, the cascade resolves to flex without needing `!important`. Same pattern v3.27.5 cleaned up on the `.btn-sm` rules at lines 8874-8876.
- **Comment block added** above the rule documenting why the flag was removed and which sibling rules participate in the cascade.
- **`!important` declaration count: 22 → 21.** Remaining 21 are all in legitimate exception buckets.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.32 / build 20260508-003 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep.

## v3.32.29
**Build:** `20260507-001` · **Released:** May 7, 2026

Three changes bundled — one cosmetic console fix, one diagnostic instrumentation pass for a long-running bug, and a laptop-viewport dot-strip overhaul.

- **#7 — `rearmLengthGuard()` `saveSession()` reorder.** `saveSession()` moved to last statement in the function (was at line 5153 before `updateLengthGuardIndicator()` / `consoleLog()` / `toast()`, now after them). The override flag round-tripped through IDB correctly either way — this is purely a console-history fix. Pre-fix, the persisted session snapshot was written before the "Length guard re-armed" console line and toast fired, so on refresh the persisted console (`consoleHTML` in IDB session) was missing the re-arm entry. Cosmetic only; verified via backup inspection 2026-05-06.
- **Satisfaction-indicator diagnostic instrumentation (gated).** The persistent `★ + NO CHANGES NEEDED` reviewer-card render bug — console correctly logs satisfaction but card sometimes shows generic `Done ✓` with no star — has resisted static analysis. The `_cleanThisRound` Set + universal re-derive + renderer rehydration architecture all check out on paper; the "sometimes" symptom suggests a timing race I can't pin down without runtime data. Adding gated logging to `setBeeStatus()`: when `window._wfSatDebug = true`, every call emits one `console.debug('[wfSat]', id, {...})` line capturing input state, set membership before/after, classList before/after, effective state after re-derive, and final summary. **OFF by default — zero perf cost.** To enable at work: open DevTools console, run `window._wfSatDebug = true`, then run the reproducing project. Filter the console with `[wfSat]` to isolate. The captured output will identify whether the bug is at the data layer (Set wrong) or render layer (DOM stale despite correct Set), and a surgical fix follows in v3.32.30 with that data in hand.
- **Laptop dot-strip overhaul (≤1600px viewport).** The compact dot-strip view (shown on smaller viewports where the full hex-card grid is hidden) gets the polish pass it's been missing.
  - **Ring width 2px → 5px (bold).** Dot diameter unchanged at 34px; inner space drops from 30px to 24px which still fits the 18px icon with 3px breathing room each side. The thicker ring carries state color more legibly at the dot's small footprint.
  - **Convergence signal added (was missing).** Prior CSS had `.bee-dot.is-builder/.is-active/.is-inactive/.is-sending/.is-working/.is-responding/.is-done/.is-error` rules but no `.bee-dot.is-clean` rule — so satisfied AIs showed identical visuals to plain "done with suggestions" AIs. Now: standard reviewer dots get **transparent background + green ring + glow + small ★ overlay** in the top-right corner when satisfied. Builder dots **keep their gold fill always** (identity layer never disappears) — the green ring and star layer on top, so a satisfied Builder reads as both "I'm the Builder" and "I'm satisfied" simultaneously. Star fade-in 150ms via opacity transition; respects `prefers-reduced-motion`.
  - **Custom hover/focus tooltip replaces native `title` attribute.** Three-line tooltip with AI name (bold), role (Builder | Reviewer), and live current state (mirrors the big-card `.hex-status` text — pulled from `blive-${id}` which is the same element `setBeeStatus` writes to). State updates **live**: `setBeeStatus` calls `refreshBeeTooltip(id)` at the end of each call when `window._wfHoveredBeeId === id`, so a hover that started during "Reviewing…" ticks over to "No changes needed ✓" the moment the state changes — no need to mouseleave/re-enter. Native `title` removed to avoid double-tooltip artifacts. Tooltip clamps to viewport so it never falls off-screen at the dot strip's edge dots; flips below the dot if above-the-dot would clip the top.
  - **Keyboard accessibility:** dots are now `tabindex="0"` and respond to `focus`/`blur` with the same tooltip behavior as `mouseenter`/`mouseleave`. `:focus-visible` ring uses the accent color so keyboard users see which dot has focus.
- **Implementation notes.** Star is a `<span class="bee-dot-star">★</span>` child of each `.bee-dot` — always present in DOM, opacity-toggled via `.is-clean` (display isn't transitionable; opacity is). Tooltip is a singleton `<div id="beeDotTooltip">` appended to `<body>` on first show, positioned in viewport coords via JS (`fixed` positioning, z-index 9999). The `:not(.is-builder)` guard on the transparent-bg rule preserves Builder gold fill — clean cascade, no `!important`.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.29 / build 20260507-001 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's `waxframe-build` meta also synced from `20260504-005` to `20260507-001`.

## v3.32.28
**Build:** `20260506-014` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Length guard reaches convergence + symmetry + visibility. Closes the three deferred items (#6b/#6c/#6d) on top of v3.32.18's per-round bloat-fail foundation. Convergence events (unanimous and majority) now consult the length constraint before celebrating; the same gate fires symmetrically for undersized output, not just overrun; and a clickable toolbar pill surfaces the override state when the user has chosen "Continue anyway · disable guard" so they can see the guard is off and re-arm it without waiting for the next prompt.

- **#6b — Convergence-path length check now fires on both unanimous and majority paths.** New helper `getLengthStatus(text)` returns `{ status: 'ok' | 'over' | 'under', actual, limitNum, floorNum, unitName, limitName }` or `null` when there's no constraint. Both convergence paths in `runRound` (unanimous at line ~11233, majority at line ~11334) now `await lengthGuardPrompt({ kind: 'convergence_over' | 'convergence_under', ... })` after the abandonment check and before `history.push`, gated on `!window._lengthGuardOverride`. `'discard'` blocks the celebration: no `history.push`, no round/phase advance, the run button is reset, the smoker overlay is hidden, status text reads "Convergence blocked — document is over/under length target/floor", and the user is left at the work screen to edit and re-run. The reviewer responses are visible in the live console — no API call is wasted, just no project-state advance. `'keep'` falls through to the existing celebration path with the guard still armed for any post-convergence iteration. `'continue_anyway'` flips `window._lengthGuardOverride = true`, calls `updateLengthGuardIndicator()`, and falls through to the celebration. The convergence pre-check is unconditional within both paths — no opt-in flag, but the override flag is the natural escape hatch.
- **#6d — Symmetric undersized guard.** New constant `LENGTH_FLOOR_RATIO = 0.5` defines the floor as half the user's length limit. Both bloat-fail blocks (`runRound` ~line 11381 and `runBuilderOnly` ~line 10770) now compute `undersizedFail` alongside `bloatFail`. Constrained branch: floor is `Math.round(limitNum * LENGTH_FLOOR_RATIO)`, with trajectory awareness symmetric to the existing bloat rule — if `prevActual < floorNum` (prior was already below floor), accept any growth (`actual > prevActual`) so a Builder conservatively building up from a near-empty starting doc isn't punished round-after-round; if `prevActual >= floorNum` and `actual < floorNum`, that's a real undersized regression and fails. Unconstrained branch: 0.5× prior-word shrinkage sanity (`newWords < prevWords * 0.5`) mirrors the existing 1.5× growth sanity. `bloatFail` and `undersizedFail` are mutually exclusive by construction. The override flag governs both directions — single project-session decision, not two separate toggles. Modal copy adapts via the new `kind` parameter ("under" vs "over"); button labels stay consistent (Discard / Keep / Continue anyway · disable guard) so muscle memory carries over from the existing bloat flow. `_failedRoundReason` distinguishes `'bloat'` from `'undersized'` for transcript clarity, and the bee status / live-console messages branch on the same.
- **#6c — Toolbar "Length guard: off" indicator.** New pill in `.work-topbar-right` (immediately before the Notes button), `id="lengthGuardIndicator"`, hidden by default via `display: none` and shown via `.is-visible` toggled by `updateLengthGuardIndicator()`. Amber palette (`rgba(245, 166, 35, ...)` matching the existing length-guard-keep button's accent so the visual language is consistent across the feature). Click handler: `rearmLengthGuard()` opens a styled `wfConfirm()` ("📏 Re-arm length guard?" / "Re-arm guard" / "Cancel"); on confirm, sets `_lengthGuardOverride = false`, calls `saveSession()`, calls `updateLengthGuardIndicator()` to hide the pill, logs to console, and toasts "📏 Length guard re-armed". Indicator render is wired into four entry points: `clearProject()` (after override reset), `loadSession()` IDB-primary path (after restore), `loadSession()` localStorage-fallback path (after restore), and `initWorkScreen()` (after `updateLicenseBadge()` — catches the page-reload sequence where loadSession's call short-circuited because the topbar wasn't yet in DOM, plus any navigation back to work mid-project). All four bloat-fail / convergence-fail `'continue_anyway'` branches also call `updateLengthGuardIndicator()` so the pill appears the moment the user disables the guard mid-project.
- **`lengthGuardPrompt({ kind, ... })` refactored to support four kinds.** `kind` defaults to `'over'` for back-compat. Recognized values: `'over'` (existing per-round bloat), `'under'` (per-round undersized), `'convergence_over'` (hive converged but doc over target), `'convergence_under'` (hive converged but doc under floor). Title, summary, button labels, distance row copy, trajectory direction icon (↓ vs ↑ flips for `under`/`convergence_under`), and help paragraph all switch on `kind`. New IDs added to `index.html`: `lengthGuardTitle`, `lengthGuardHelp`, `lengthGuardDiscardBtn`, `lengthGuardKeepBtn`, `lengthGuardContinueBtn`. The Prior-document and Trajectory rows hide via a new `.length-guard-row.is-hidden` CSS class for the convergence kinds, where there's no round-to-round comparison applicable — the gate is on the standalone document length.
- **No data-model changes.** The `lengthGuardOverride` field added to the IDB session payload in v3.32.18 is reused as-is; no schema bump, no migration. v3 backups created before v3.32.28 import correctly because the new code paths only consult the existing flag and the new `LENGTH_FLOOR_RATIO` constant is computed locally from the existing `lengthLimit` UI input. Pre-v3.32.28 sessions reload with the override flag at whatever value they had when last saved.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.28 / build 20260506-014 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-013` to `20260506-014`.

## v3.32.27
**Build:** `20260506-013` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Visual half of the build-phase satisfaction-star flicker. v3.32.26 fixed the data-side: removed the `if (state === 'sending') window._cleanThisRound.delete(id);` side-effect from `setBeeStatus`, so a satisfied builder stayed tracked as satisfied through the build phase. But the visual still flickered because the CSS class controlling the rendered star (`is-clean`) was getting wiped by the unconditional `card.classList.remove(...allStates)` at the top of every `setBeeStatus` call, and the universal re-derive that re-applies it only fires for non-`'sending'` states.

- **`is-clean` class now persists during `'sending'` transitions when the AI is in `_cleanThisRound`.** New line in the `'sending'` branch: `if (window._cleanThisRound.has(id)) add('is-clean');`. This lets the green border + ★ visual coexist with the pulsing `is-sending` animation when the Builder transitions to `'Building…'` mid-round. CSS source order resolves the border-color conflict in favor of green (is-clean rule lives later in the stylesheet than is-sending), the pulsing animation from is-sending is on a different property so it still runs, the star comes from `.hex-cell.is-clean .hex-clean-star { display: flex }`, and the status text stays blue "BUILDING…" because `.hex-cell.is-sending .hex-status { color: var(--blue) }` has no is-clean override.
- **What the satisfied builder card now shows during build phase:** green border, blue pulsing animation, star at far right, "BUILDING…" status text in blue. "This AI was satisfied this round" anchor persists through the build instead of flickering off and back on. Non-satisfied builders are unchanged — they don't enter the `_cleanThisRound.has(id)` branch, so they show the standard is-sending visual (blue border + animation, no star).
- **No CSS changes needed.** The cascade was already correct for the case where both classes are present; it just needed the JS to actually set both classes simultaneously in this transition.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.27 / build 20260506-013 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep. Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-012` to `20260506-013`.

---
## v3.32.26
**Build:** `20260506-012` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Three state-management fixes shipped together. All three are "user makes a change, change works for the session, refresh or mid-round transition loses it" — the canonical session-persistence regression class. The unifying theme: state mutations needed an explicit save or were being clobbered by a now-redundant side-effect.

- **`setBuilder()` now calls `saveHive()`.** `saveHive()` already includes the `builder` field in its localStorage payload (line 2725) — the bug was that the setter only updated the in-memory variable and never called the persist function. Result: the user changes the Builder via the Change Builder modal, it works for the session, then a refresh resets to whatever was loaded at boot. `setBuilderFromModal()` routes through `setBuilder()`, so the modal flow now persists too. `loadHive()` at boot already restores `builder = h.builder`, so the round trip is now complete.
- **`sessionAIs` Set joins the IDB session payload.** Previously the per-session AI checkbox toggles on the work-screen hive cards were tracked in `window.sessionAIs` and never persisted anywhere. Worse, `initWorkScreen()` line 8671 unconditionally re-initialized the Set to all-active-AIs every time the work screen rendered, including on page reload. So even if persistence had been added, the reset would have wiped it before any UI saw it. Three pieces of plumbing: (1) `saveSession()` serializes `sessionAIs: Array.from(window.sessionAIs || [])` alongside the existing `cleanThisRound` field; (2) `loadSession()` restores it on both primary IDB and localStorage fallback paths via `if (Array.isArray(s.sessionAIs)) window.sessionAIs = new Set(s.sessionAIs)` — pre-v3.32.26 sessions leave `window.sessionAIs` undefined so the reset path takes over, preserving historical behavior; (3) `initWorkScreen()`'s reset is now gated `if (isNewSession || !window.sessionAIs)` so only true new sessions and brand-new installs get the all-on default. (4) `toggleSessionBee()` calls `saveSession()` after each toggle so a quick toggle followed by refresh persists immediately rather than waiting for the next round / doc edit / notes save to flush.
- **Build-phase satisfaction-star flicker fixed.** Removed the `if (state === 'sending') window._cleanThisRound.delete(id);` side-effect from `setBeeStatus`. The bug: a satisfied reviewer (e.g. ChatGPT returns NO CHANGES NEEDED, gets `is-clean` star) that is ALSO the Builder for the round would transition to `'sending'` with summary `'Building…'` when the build phase started, the side-effect would delete it from `_cleanThisRound`, the star would visibly disappear, then `runRound`'s `builderWasClean` re-promotion path at build-completion would re-add it and the star would reappear. Visible "★ → gone during build → ★" cycle every round where the same AI was both clean and Builder. The side-effect was redundant for its only correct use case (smoke-phase reset) because `runRound` already does an explicit `if (window._cleanThisRound) window._cleanThisRound.clear();` at line 10936 (added in v3.32.14) at round-start. Removing the side-effect leaves the explicit clear as the sole authority over the Set's lifecycle, eliminates the flicker, and tightens the state machine — the chokepoint no longer touches the Set on `'sending'` transitions, only on `'done-clean'` adds.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.26 / build 20260506-012 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep. Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-011` to `20260506-012`.

## v3.32.25
**Build:** `20260506-011` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Single-line CSS tweak. The v3.32.23 satisfaction star had `padding: 0 12px` which produced 12px of margin from the star glyph to the card's right edge. The card's left side has 8px of body padding from the colored state strip to the first content (the 4px strip itself reads as state ornament rather than empty margin), so the right-side margin perceptually looked 4px bigger than the left.

- **`.hex-clean-star { padding: 0 12px }` → `padding: 0 8px`.** Matches the `.hex-cell-body`'s 8px left padding so the visible margin from the star glyph to the card's right edge equals the visible margin from the colored state strip to the first body content. Both sides now read as 8px symmetric margins.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.25 / build 20260506-011 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep. Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-010` to `20260506-011`.

## v3.32.24
**Build:** `20260506-010` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Bug fix: per-round satisfaction state now survives page reloads. The `window._cleanThisRound` Set tracks which AIs returned NO CHANGES NEEDED for the current round and drives the green-border + ★ rendering. v3.32.14 added a rehydration path in `renderBeeStatusGrid` that walks the Set and re-applies `is-clean` state on each card after `innerHTML` rebuild — but the Set itself was never persisted to IDB. On page reload the Set was reinitialized empty (line 10174's `if (!window._cleanThisRound) window._cleanThisRound = new Set();`), the rehydration walked an empty Set, and previously-satisfied cards lost their star until the next round produced new satisfaction signals. The fix mirrors what v3.32.18 did for `_lengthGuardOverride`.

- **`saveSession()` now serializes `_cleanThisRound` to the IDB session payload** as `cleanThisRound: Array.from(window._cleanThisRound || [])`. Sets aren't JSON-friendly so the array form is what gets persisted.
- **`loadSession()` now restores it on both paths** — primary IDB path and localStorage fallback path. Reconstructs the Set from the serialized array via `window._cleanThisRound = new Set(Array.isArray(s.cleanThisRound) ? s.cleanThisRound : [])`. Defaults to an empty Set for pre-v3.32.24 sessions where the field doesn't exist (`!Array.isArray` guard).
- **`clearProject()` still clears the Set** at line 10884 — that path was already correct, no change needed. The IDB wipe also clears the persisted value.
- **No data-model migration needed.** Pre-v3.32.24 sessions just hydrate to an empty Set; the next round's `setBeeStatus('done-clean', ...)` calls populate it normally and from that point forward it persists.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.24 / build 20260506-010 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep. Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-009` to `20260506-010`.

---
## v3.32.23
**Build:** `20260506-009` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Architectural change to the satisfaction star. v3.32.21–22 placed the star inline on row 2 with `margin-left: auto`, which constrained it to single-row height and put it in the same horizontal flow as status text. v3.32.23 promotes the star to a card-level affordance: sibling of `.hex-cell-body`, vertically centered across both rows, sized large enough to span the combined row heights as a visual anchor.

- **Star moved out of `.hex-row.hex-row-status` into `.hex-cell` directly.** The `<span class="hex-clean-star">★</span>` is now a sibling of `.hex-cell-body` in the card markup. Since `.hex-cell` is already `display: flex; flex-direction: row; align-items: stretch`, the star span naturally stretches to full card height; `display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center` on the span itself centers the glyph vertically within that stretched footprint.
- **Star size bumped from 18px to 28px.** Roughly matches the combined height of the two rows (16+16+gap), so the star reads as a visual anchor at the card's right edge rather than a small inline accent. `line-height: 1` keeps the glyph's vertical footprint matched to its rendered size; no more glyph-baseline tightening needed since the star isn't competing with row text for height.
- **`.hex-cell-body` shrinks via existing flex behavior to leave room.** No new rules required — `.hex-cell-body` already has `flex: 1; min-width: 0` so it gives up width to the star (`flex-shrink: 0` on `.hex-clean-star` keeps it at full size). The `.hex-name` (row 1) and `.hex-status` (row 2) already have `text-overflow: ellipsis` so long names and long status messages truncate cleanly when the body width contracts. Long names like `[Base] Claude-3-7-Sonnet` (already display-stripped to `Claude-3-7-Sonnet` via `displayAiName()`) ellipsis as expected when the star is present and don't when it isn't.
- **`padding: 0 12px` on the star** gives the glyph breathing room from the card's right edge (matching the body's 8px right padding plus a touch more so the star doesn't crowd content).
- **Light-theme color override unchanged** — still `#d97706` with full opacity for visibility on light backgrounds.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.23 / build 20260506-009 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep. Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-008` to `20260506-009`.

---
## v3.32.22
**Build:** `20260506-008` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Single-fix release. The v3.32.21 inline satisfaction star (`.hex-clean-star`) on row 2 of the bee card was sized at 14px, which numerically should have read as larger than the BUILDER pill (8px) and status text (9px) it sits next to — but the serif ★ glyph has built-in whitespace padding inside its em-box, so its perceived visual mass was smaller than its declared font-size. The star looked smaller than the surrounding text in real use even though the font-size value said otherwise.

- **`.hex-clean-star` font-size bumped 14px → 18px** so the star reads as visually larger than the BUILDER pill and status text instead of appearing equal-or-smaller. The 4px bump compensates for the glyph's internal whitespace padding without overwhelming the row.
- **`line-height` tightened from 1 to 0.8** to pull the glyph baseline up so it sits proportionate to the row's text height. Without this, bumping font-size alone would add extra vertical space to row 2 that the card's compact layout doesn't have. With `line-height: 0.8`, the star's perceived visual mass grows but its row footprint stays compact.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.22 / build 20260506-008 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep. Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-007` to `20260506-008`.

## v3.32.21
**Build:** `20260506-007` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Six fixes shipped together — five surface from real-use feedback on v3.32.20, plus the long-running header truncation issue at laptop widths. The unifying theme: tighten the visual contracts that v3.32.20 broke or didn't address.

- **BUILDER text pill restored to the checkbox slot in row 1.** v3.32.20 placed the Builder Bee PNG (`WaxFrame_Builder_v3.png`) in the row-1 slot where non-builder cards have their checkbox. The slot's natural footprint at ~13–14px made the bee silhouette unreadable mush — at small sizes the bee asset doesn't communicate "Builder," it just looks like noise. Reverted to the prior plaintext "BUILDER" amber chip (`.hex-builder-tag`) that reads at any size and matches the slot dimensions naturally. The bee asset is preserved for places with room (Setup 2 picker, Change Builder modal title) where it has the visual budget to be recognizable.
- **Satisfaction star moved from centered overlay to inline at row-2's far right.** The prior `.hex-cell.is-clean::after` rule rendered the ★ as a 28px absolute-positioned overlay centered on the whole card, which obscured row content. New `.hex-clean-star` inline element on row 2 with `margin-left: auto` pushes it to the row's far right. The `.hex-cell.is-clean` class still controls visibility (display: none → inline) so the rendering path is unchanged from `setBeeStatus`'s perspective; only the visual positioning moved. Same gold-amber color and serif star character so the visual association is preserved.
- **`setBeeStatus` now respects the `summary` arg for the 'sending' state.** Was hardcoded to `live.textContent = 'Sending…'` regardless of what summary was passed. Both call sites pass meaningful summaries — `'Building…'` for the Builder during the build phase (via `runBuilderOnly` and `runRound`) and `'Reviewing…'` for reviewers during the smoke phase (via the `allReviewers.forEach` in `runRound`) — and those have been silently lost since the function was written. Now the passed summary is honored; `'Sending…'` only used as a fallback when no summary was provided. Effect: Builder card correctly reads "BUILDING…" during build phase; reviewer cards correctly read "REVIEWING…" during smoke phase. Two intended behaviors reach the screen for the first time.
- **Header truncation at laptop widths via `max-width: calc(50% - 150px)` on `.work-topbar-left`.** Long project names like "CJS Resume - Program Coordinator, Air Dominance & Strike" were visually overflowing the absolute-positioned `.work-topbar-center` round badge zone at viewport widths around 1422px, jamming all three header zones together. Math: half-of-topbar minus half-of-badge-width minus a small safety gap. The Round badge at 17px monospace font with 56px horizontal padding ≈ 280px max width, so half is ~140px; using 150px gives a 10px buffer. The existing `.work-project-name { text-overflow: ellipsis }` rule then activates correctly as the name competes for the constrained left-zone width. At wider viewports this still leaves plenty of room for full names; at laptop widths it cleanly truncates instead of overlapping.
- **Change Builder modal switched from `.builder-pick-grid-large` to `.builder-pick-grid`** plus modal width bumped from 560px to 760px. Architectural mismatch: the `-large` variant was tuned in v3.27.7/v3.27.9 for the Setup 2 screen with ~470–540px wide cards (3-column grid at 1fr each, 56px icons, 28px padding, aspect-ratio 2/1). When dropped into a 560px-max-width modal, those same sizings collapsed cards to ~168px wide where 56px icons couldn't fit and aspect-ratio constraints made the cards too short for content. The base `.builder-pick-grid` (flex-wrap layout with 100px min-width per card, 36px icons, 20px padding) was designed for compact contexts and is the correct fit for a modal. Modal max-width raised to 760px so 7 AIs in a flex-wrap layout get ~140–160px per card instead of squeezing to ~100px.
- **👑 crown emoji replaced with Builder Bee PNG in modal titles and persistent buttons.** Three sites swapped: Change Builder modal title (`<h2 class="finish-modal-title">`), Round Error modal's "Change Builder" button, and the Change Builder modal's "Current" indicator on the selected card. New small CSS classes (`.modal-title-bee` 22px, `.modal-btn-bee` 18px, `.builder-pick-current-bee` 12px) with consistent vertical alignment baselines (object-fit: contain, vertical-align: middle, small negative margin-top to sit centered on x-height). The bee asset is the same `images/WaxFrame_Builder_v3.png` used by the Setup 2 picker. Toast notifications (`👑 Builder changed to ...`) deliberately keep the emoji per the project's transient-vs-persistent affordance convention — toasts are flash-and-gone, emoji works there; titles and buttons are persistent, bee belongs.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.21 / build 20260506-007 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep. Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-006` to `20260506-007`.

---
## v3.32.20
**Build:** `20260506-006` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Closes punch-list item **#8** — the hive card layout redesign. The previous single-row layout crammed up to 6 elements (checkbox, BUILDER tag, provider icon, AI name, live status, satisfaction star overlay) into one horizontal line; long names like `[Base] Claude-3-7-Sonnet` truncated mid-word ("Geh…") on cards that also carried the BUILDER tag because everything competed for the same row's horizontal space. v3.32.20 splits the card into two stacked rows with distinct jobs, drops the visually-redundant `[Base]` prefix from card display, shrinks provider icons to 16×16 to fit the row-1 vertical space, and replaces the prior plaintext "BUILDER" pill with the Builder Bee PNG used elsewhere in the app.

- **Two-row card body in `renderBeeStatusGrid()`.** Row 1 (`.hex-row-identity`) carries identity: the optional checkbox (non-builders only — builders are always-on so they don't get a toggle), the provider icon at 16×16, and the AI name. Row 2 (`.hex-row-status`) carries status: the Builder Bee role indicator (only on the Builder card) and the live status text. Splitting these into separate rows means the AI name has the full row-1 width to itself and never collides horizontally with the role indicator or status pills, which was the structural cause of the prior truncation.
- **`.hex-cell-body` now flexes column instead of row** with `gap: 2px` between rows and `padding: 4px 8px` (down from `5px 8px`) — the prior single-row body had generous vertical padding that's now absorbed into the second row, so total card height stays the same. `min-height: 44px` on `.hex-cell` is unchanged. New helper classes `.hex-row`, `.hex-row-identity`, `.hex-row-status` provide the row containers; `.hex-row-status` carries a slight `opacity: 0.95` dim so the eye lands on identity first, then status.
- **`displayAiName()` helper strips `[Base] ` prefix from card display only.** New small util near `esc()`. Used exclusively in the work-screen card markup. Underlying `ai.name` stays unchanged so export, transcript, conflict attribution, and the Change Builder modal (where the Base-vs-Custom distinction is useful at-a-glance) all continue to see the prefixed form. The card's `title` attribute still carries the full `ai.name` so hover reveals the underlying value.
- **`.hex-icon` shrunk from 20×20 to 16×16** to fit the row-1 vertical space in the two-row layout. `.hex-icon-avatar` (the letter-fallback when a provider PNG fails to load) tracks the same size so the avatar fallback stays visually identical to the loaded-icon case. Source PNGs are 256×256 and stay that size — CSS sizing is local to this card context.
- **Builder Bee role indicator replaces the plaintext `.hex-builder-tag` pill.** New `.hex-builder-bee` rule (14×14 PNG, `flex-shrink: 0`) renders `images/WaxFrame_Builder_v3.png` — the same asset used by the Setup 2 picker's `.builder-selected-badge`. Visual consistency: "this bee = Builder role" reads the same across screens. Old `.hex-builder-tag` CSS rule retired (the only other reference was the now-rewritten card markup); the comment in `.hex-builder-bee` notes the replacement for archaeology.
- **Status row width handling.** `.hex-status` switched from `flex-shrink: 0` to `flex: 1; min-width: 0` so longer status messages ("No changes proposed · Built doc ✓", "Length limit exceeded (170%)", etc.) get full row-2 width with ellipsis fallback when truncation is genuinely required, instead of being squeezed off-screen by the fixed-shrink.
- **Satisfaction-star overlay (`.hex-cell.is-clean::after`) untouched.** The big centered ★ that overlays the whole card when an AI is satisfied is positioned on `.hex-cell` (not `.hex-cell-body`), so it continues to render in the new layout exactly as it did in the old one. The known intermittent rendering bug — where satisfaction is correctly logged in console but the card sometimes shows generic DONE without the star — is **not** fixed in this release; it remains the next dedicated bug-fix release per the existing punch-list note.
- **Laptop variant (≤1600px) unaffected.** At laptop width the full hex-grid is hidden via `display: none` and the dot-strip view takes over — that's a separate visual treatment that doesn't share markup with the redesigned cards. No changes to the dot-strip path.
- **Punch list housekeeping (no code change):** Item **#2** (BUILDER badge missing on hive card — 3.32.14 regression) closed as resolved. Per David, the regression is no longer reproducing in current builds; likely fixed as a side effect of the v3.32.16 icon-path consolidation that routed all BUILDER-related rendering through `resolveAiIcon`. v3.32.20's complete rewrite of the BUILDER role rendering (now the Builder Bee PNG instead of the prior plaintext tag) makes the original failure mode structurally impossible going forward.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.20 / build 20260506-006 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `app.js` `BUILD`, `index.html` `<meta name="waxframe-build">`, `index.html` `app.js?v=` cache-bust) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-005` to `20260506-006`. The `style.css?v=` and `js/version.js?v=` cache-busts in `index.html` itself caught at the same time.

## v3.32.19
**Build:** `20260506-005` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Closes punch-list item #5 — the Change Builder modal CSS. The malformed-HTML half (double `class=` attribute) was fixed in v3.32.16; this release closes the remaining text-overflow issue where long AI names like `[Base] Claude-3-7-Sonnet` extended past their card boundaries and visually collided with neighboring cards in the grid.

- **`.builder-pick-btn` gets `overflow: hidden`.** Defensive safety net so a long name can never visually escape its card, regardless of which render path produced the markup. Pairs with the wrap behavior on `.builder-pick-name` below — wrap is the primary mechanism, clip is the fallback for unbreakable tokens or extreme widths.
- **`.builder-pick-name` gets proper text wrapping.** Adds `word-break: break-word` so hyphenated model names like `Claude-3-7-Sonnet` or `GPT-4o-Mini` break cleanly at hyphen boundaries; `overflow-wrap: anywhere` as a fallback for unbreakable tokens (extremely long single words with no break opportunities); `max-width: 100%` to keep names within the card's content box; `line-height: 1.25` for multi-line breathing room without making single-line names feel loose; `text-align: center` to match the column layout's centered alignment so wrapped names remain visually centered.
- **`title="${esc(ai.name)}"` added to both Builder picker render paths.** `openChangeBuilder()` (Change Builder modal — small variant, 36px icon) and `renderBuilderPicker()` (Setup 2 grid — large variant, 56px icon). Provides a hover affordance so the user can see the full name even when the visible name is wrapped or clipped at the card edge. `esc()` keeps any quote characters in custom AI names from breaking the attribute.
- **`aspect-ratio` constraint deliberately preserved.** Considered replacing it with `min-height` so cards could grow vertically when names wrap, but the proportional scaling David tuned in v3.27.7/v3.27.9/v3.27.10/v3.27.11 produces a deliberate visual rhythm — cards at ~470×235 (laptop) and ~540×270 (full desktop) with content centered in the available space. Replacing `aspect-ratio` with `min-height` would break that. The wrap + clip + title combination handles long names gracefully without changing the grid's visual proportions.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.19 / build 20260506-005 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `app.js` `BUILD`, `index.html` `<meta name="waxframe-build">`, `index.html` `app.js?v=` cache-bust) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-004` to `20260506-005`. The `style.css?v=` and `js/version.js?v=` cache-busts in `index.html` itself caught at the same time.

**Punch list housekeeping (no code change):** Item **#3** (`WaxFrame_Playbook_Round_Count_Tests.txt` reportedly stale) closed as not-an-issue — the file isn't in the deployed repo. It was a local-only scratch file that was never deployed, so there's nothing in the published site or repo to clean up.

## v3.32.18
**Build:** `20260506-004` · **Released:** May 6, 2026

Closes punch-list item #6 — the length-band guard's silent-discard loop. When the user pasted a starting document already over the length limit, every round was rejected as bloat regardless of whether the round made progress, and the user had no way to break out short of changing the limit or clearing the project. v3.32.18 makes the guard trajectory-aware and adds a per-round modal interrupt with three explicit choices.

- **Trajectory awareness in the constrained length gate.** Both `runRound()` and `runBuilderOnly()` now compute `prevActual` (prior document size in the user's chosen unit) alongside the existing `actual` (this round's output size). When `prevActual > limitNum` (the prior doc was already over the limit), `bloatFail` is now `actual >= prevActual` — the round only fails if the Builder didn't move size DOWN. When `prevActual <= limitNum` (the prior doc was within target), the existing strict ceiling applies (`actual > limitNum`). This kills the convergence-down loop where every round was rejected because each was still over the absolute ceiling, even though each was strictly smaller than the last. Unconstrained 1.5×-prior-words sanity check (the fallback path when no length is set) is unchanged — trajectory awareness has no meaningful target there.
- **Per-round length-guard modal: `lengthGuardPrompt()`.** When the trajectory-aware gate would still fail AND the user has a length constraint set, a new modal interrupts the round with three explicit choices instead of silently saving as failed: **Discard round** (default conservative — same as the existing BUILDER_BLOAT failed-round path), **Keep this round** (accept the over-limit doc as the new CURRENT, but the guard stays active for future rounds — one-time override), or **Continue anyway · disable guard** (accept AND set `window._lengthGuardOverride = true` for the rest of the project session — useful when the guard's target genuinely doesn't match what the document needs to be). Modal shows trajectory info — prior length, this round's length, target limit, direction (↓ improving green, ↑ worsening red, unchanged dim), and absolute distance from target. Returns a Promise that resolves to `'discard' | 'keep' | 'continue_anyway'`. Unconstrained 1.5× sanity-check failures take the existing failed-round path because there's no real "target" to override against.
- **`window._lengthGuardOverride` flag with IDB-session persistence.** New session-scoped flag. `false` by default. Set to `true` when the user picks "Continue anyway" from the length-guard modal. Persisted in the IDB session payload alongside `round`, `phase`, `history`, `docText`, `consoleHTML`, `notes`, and `projClockSeconds`. Restored on `loadSession()` (both primary IDB path and localStorage fallback path) so it survives page reloads mid-project. Reset to `false` explicitly in `clearProject()` so a fresh project always starts with the guard armed — the IDB wipe also clears the persisted value, but resetting the window global explicitly avoids any race where the next round in a new project sees the stale value before the first save runs. When the flag is `true`, the gate check at both bloat sites short-circuits with a console log noting the override is active and falls through to the success path.
- **No data-model changes beyond the override flag.** No new icon fields, no two-icon schema. v3 backups created before v3.32.18 import correctly — `lengthGuardOverride` defaults to `false` when the field isn't present (`!!s.lengthGuardOverride`).
- **Deferred to a future release** (now tracked as punch list #6b/#6c/#6d): convergence-path length checks (block unanimous/majority convergence while CURRENT is over limit unless override is set), a visual "Length guard: off" indicator on the work-screen toolbar surfacing the override state to the user mid-session, and a min-length / undersized-target symmetric guard. All three are additive on top of v3.32.18's foundation and don't change the data model further. Scope kept tight here so this release stays surgical and verifiable.
- **Version stamps in code bumped** to v3.32.18 / build 20260506-004 across the canonical 4-stamp checklist (`version.js` `APP_VERSION`, `app.js` `BUILD`, `index.html` `<meta name="waxframe-build">`, `index.html` `app.js?v=` cache-bust) plus the full 6-file cache-bust sweep covering `index.html` and the 5 helper pages (`waxframe-user-manual.html`, `document-playbooks.html`, `what-are-tokens.html`, `api-details.html`, `prompt-editor.html`). Each helper page's comment-header build stamp also synced from `20260506-003` to `20260506-004`. The `style.css?v=` and `js/version.js?v=` cache-busts in `index.html` itself caught at the same time.
